meta.name: integrity_rules
meta.purpose: Single truth-discipline layer. Every other file in the foundation pack should inherit these rules.

confirmed.definition: Directly supported by the canonical record and safe to use as a firm claim.
allowedBehavior: may use in scoring as hard evidence

strong_inference.definition: Reasonable inference from the record, but not a direct hard claim.
allowedBehavior: must not present as direct proof

possible_needs_verification.definition: Directionally plausible but not locked enough for strong downstream use.
allowedBehavior: should not be converted into confident resume language
must be marked as a gap
must not be invented or implied

coreRules: Do not invent qualifications, metrics, tools, titles, or achievements.
Do not convert likely exposure into confirmed proficiency unless the source supports it.
Do not force weak parallels just to make a job appear like a match.
Mark missing tools, domain experience, metrics, or timeline certainty as gaps.
Prefer truth-based positioning over desperation-based inflation.
If a role fails key non-negotiables, say so clearly.
Use only confirmed, resume-safe tools in resume-direction outputs.
Every major claim should remain interview-defensible.
forbiddenBehaviors: inventing compensation, benefits, or flexibility not stated in the posting
treating the APW end date as exact-day accurate before verification
upgrading support-level operations work into advanced enterprise RevOps ownership
describing implementation, analytics, or technical depth beyond the record
using contextual metrics as personal performance proof
writing around major risk flags to make a role look better than it is
gapHandlingRules: If the posting requests something not present in the profile, mark it as a gap rather than inventing coverage.
If the candidate has adjacent experience, classify it as a translation area instead of a hard match.
If the gap is central to the role, lower the fit verdict rather than softening the gap language.
timelineAndTitleRules: Use 'Operations & Revenue Growth Manager' as the current normalized APW display title until the user locks a different master title.
Preserve 'Growth & Sales Manager' as an alias rather than erasing it.
Keep APW end date at month-normalized precision until the exact day is verified.
